finegym|UB_transition_flight_start.general: The moment the gymnast begins a flight element on the uneven bars, releasing from the bar to enter an airborne phase.

finegym|FX_front_salto_start.general: A forward somersault on the floor exercise begins as the gymnast initiates the airborne rotation by pushing off the ground.

finegym|UB_fligh_same_bar_end.general: A movement where a gymnast travels from one part of the uneven bars to another without releasing the bar, ending when the hands or body stop moving along the bar or land on the same bar.

The moment a same-bar flight action on uneven bars finishes, defined by the gymnast's final position or landing on the bar.

finediving|Som(s).Tuck.general: The moment a diver begins a tucked somersault, initiating the aerial rotation with the body in a tucked position.
paraphrases: The instant the diver starts a tucked somersault, marking the onset of the aerial phase.
The point at which a tucked somersault is initiated, with the body folding into a tuck.
The moment the diver launches into a tucked somersault, beginning the rotation.
The start of a tucked somersault, where the diver enters the aerial phase in a tucked body shape.
The onset of a tucked somersault, characterized by the diver pulling into a tuck as rotation begins.
The instant the aerial phase of a tucked somersault begins, with the diver's body in a tucked position.
The moment the diver initiates a tucked somersault, marking the beginning of the rotation.
The start of the tucked somersault, where the diver leaves the platform or board and begins the aerial rotation.

tennis|near_court_bounce.general: The instant the tennis ball strikes the court surface on the near side of the court.
paraphrases: The moment when the tennis ball contacts the court on the near side.
The frame at which the ball bounces on the near half of the court.
The point in time when the ball hits the court surface near the camera.
The instant the ball makes contact with the ground on the court's near side.
The moment the tennis ball lands and bounces on the near side of the court.
The frame capturing the ball striking the court surface on the near side.
The instant the ball touches the court floor on the side closest to the camera.
The moment the ball bounces off the court surface on the near side.

finegym|FX_leap_jump_hop_start.general: On the floor exercise, the moment a leap, jump, or hop begins is the instant the gymnast initiates the takeoff from the ground, transitioning from support to airborne flight.
paraphrases: In floor exercise, the start of a leap, jump, or hop occurs when the gymnast first leaves the floor to become airborne.
The beginning of a floor exercise leap, jump, or hop is marked by the gymnast's initial push-off from the mat.
For a leap, jump, or hop on floor, the onset is the exact point when the gymnast's feet lose contact with the ground.
The start of a floor exercise airborne step—leap, jump, or hop—is the takeoff moment when the gymnast propels upward.
In floor routine, the initiation of a leap, jump, or hop is the instant the gymnast's foot leaves the floor.
The beginning frame of a floor exercise leap, jump, or hop captures the gymnast starting the upward motion from the ground.
A floor exercise leap, jump, or hop starts when the gymnast begins the takeoff, ending ground contact.
The onset of a leap, jump, or hop on floor is the moment the gymnast pushes off the mat to enter the air.

touchmoment|touch.general: The moment a gymnast's hand first makes contact with an apparatus, such as the beam, bars, or floor, during a routine.
paraphrases: The instant a gymnast's hand touches the apparatus for the first time.
The moment when the hand initially contacts the beam, bars, or floor.
The point at which the gymnast's hand first makes contact with the equipment.
The moment the hand reaches and touches the apparatus during a skill.
The instant of initial hand contact with the beam, bars, or floor surface.
The moment when the gymnast's hand first makes physical contact with the apparatus.
The point in time when the hand first touches the equipment in a routine.
The instant of first hand contact with the apparatus, marking the beginning of a support or grip.

finediving|Som(s).Pike.general: The moment a diver initiates the aerial phase of a somersault in pike position, marking the start of the rotation around the transverse axis as they leave the board or platform.
paraphrases: The instant a diver starts a piked somersault in the air, beginning the rotation from the takeoff.
The onset of the aerial phase of a somersault with the body folded at the hips (pike), as the dive begins.
The moment the diver launches into a pike-position somersault, initiating the rotational motion.
The start of the airborne somersault in pike position, immediately after the diver pushes off the board or platform.
The beginning of a piked somersault, where the diver first leaves the board and starts the forward or backward rotation.
The point at which the diver transitions from takeoff to the piked somersault, commencing the aerial rotation.
The instant the diver's body assumes the pike shape and the somersault rotation begins in midair.
The onset of the dive's somersault phase, with the body in pike position, as the diver becomes airborne.

finegym|FX_back_salto_start.general: A floor exercise backward salto begins when the gymnast initiates the backward rotation by pushing off the floor and driving the arms upward.
paraphrases: The start of a backward salto on floor occurs at the instant the gymnast presses off the mat and starts the backward somersault.
On floor exercise, a backward salto commences as the gymnast pushes from the floor and begins rotating backward.
The onset of a floor backward salto is marked by the gymnast's takeoff from the floor and the initiation of the backward flip.
A backward salto on floor exercise starts when the gymnast jumps upward and backward, starting the somersault motion.
The beginning of a backward salto on floor is the moment the gymnast's feet leave the floor and the backward rotation begins.
On floor exercise, the initial frame of a backward salto shows the gymnast pushing off the floor and starting the backward somersault.
A floor backward salto begins at the takeoff, when the gymnast drives off the floor and rotates backward.
The start of a backward salto on the floor exercise is the instant the gymnast leaves the ground and begins the backward aerial rotation.

finegym|BB_turns_end.general: The moment a turn on the balance beam finishes, when the gymnast ceases rotation and stabilizes in the final position, typically with the supporting foot on the beam.
paraphrases: The instant a turn on the balance beam ends, as the gymnast stops spinning and holds the concluding stance, usually with one foot on the beam.
The frame marking the completion of a balance beam turn, where the rotation halts and the body settles into the ending posture, often with the standing foot on the apparatus.
When a turn on the balance beam concludes: the rotation stops and the gymnast achieves a controlled final alignment, generally with the supporting foot contacting the beam.
The endpoint of a balance beam turn, characterized by the cessation of rotational motion and the assumption of a stable final shape, typically with the foot planted on the beam.
The moment a turn on the balance beam is finished, as the gymnast ends the rotation and freezes in the final pose, usually with the standing foot on the beam.
The conclusion of a balance beam turn, where the gymnast completes the spin and holds the ending position, often with the foot placed on the beam.
The instant a turn on the balance beam terminates, marked by the end of rotation and the establishment of a static final position, typically with the supporting foot on the beam.
The frame when a balance beam turn ends: the rotation finishes and the gymnast stabilizes in the final stance, usually with one foot on the beam.

finegym|BB_flight_salto_start.general: The moment a somersault begins on the balance beam.
paraphrases: The instant a somersault is initiated on the balance beam.
The onset of a somersault on the balance beam.
The start of a salto on the balance beam.
The frame where a somersault commences on the balance beam.
The beginning of a somersault on the balance beam.
The moment the gymnast starts a somersault on the balance beam.
The initiation of a somersault on the balance beam.
The point at which a somersault starts on the balance beam.

finegym|VT_2.general: The moment the gymnast pushes off the vaulting table during the first flight phase of a vault.
paraphrases: The instant when the gymnast's hands contact and push against the vault table after the initial airborne phase.
The moment of forceful hand contact with the vault table following the run and first flight.
The phase-instant where the gymnast drives off the vaulting table with both hands.
The point in the vault when the gymnast executes the table push after the first flight.
The moment the gymnast's hands strike the vault table and propel the body into the second flight.
The instant of hand-to-table contact that marks the transition from first flight to second flight.
The moment the gymnast pushes off the vault table, generating upward and rotational momentum.
The phase-instant of the vault where the gymnast's hands leave the table after the push.

finegym|VT_3.general: The moment a gymnast pushes off the vaulting table, transitioning from the first flight to the second flight.
paraphrases: The instant when the gymnast's hands leave the vaulting table after the block.
The point at which the gymnast exerts force on the table to launch into the post-flight phase.
The moment of repulsion from the vaulting table, ending the table contact.
The split second the gymnast pushes away from the table, beginning the airborne second flight.
The phase-instant of the table push, where the gymnast transfers momentum from the first flight to the second flight.
The moment the gymnast's body is propelled upward and forward off the vaulting table.
The instant of the block, marking the transition from the table support to free flight.
The point in the vault when the gymnast's hands separate from the table, initiating the second flight.
